name: World Knowledge
description: Geography, world facts, and international knowledge -- some with a South African twist!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: Which continent is South Africa on?
questions.explanation: Come on now -- it's right there in the name! South AFRICA is on the continent of Africa. Easiest one you'll ever get!

questions.question: Which ocean borders South Africa to the east?
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is on SA's east coast (Durban side). The Atlantic is on the west (Cape Town side). Basic geography, that!

questions.question: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital of Japan. Over 13 million people live there -- that's more than the whole of Gauteng!

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: Seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia/Oceania, Europe, North America, and South America. Lucky 7!

questions.question: Which is the largest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Russia is massive -- over 17 million square kilometres! That's about 14 times the size of South Africa. Absolutely enormous.

questions.question: Which country has the most people in the world?
questions.explanation: India overtook China in 2023 as the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion people. That's about 23 times SA's population!

questions.question: The Sahara Desert is located on which continent?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is in northern Africa -- it's the largest hot desert in the world. Makes the Karoo look like a garden!

questions.question: How many countries share a land border with South Africa?
questions.explanation: SA borders Namibia, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Mozambique, Eswatini, and Lesotho (which is completely surrounded by SA!). That's 6 neighbours.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile at about 6,650 km is generally considered the longest river. It flows through northeast Africa. The Amazon is close though -- there's actually ongoing debate!

questions.question: In which city would you find the Eiffel Tower?
questions.explanation: The Eiffel Tower is in Paris, France. Built in 1889, it's 330 metres tall. Quite a bit taller than the Hillbrow Tower!

questions.question: Which African country was never colonised by a European power?
questions.explanation: Ethiopia (along with Liberia) was never formally colonised. Italy tried to invade in 1896 but was defeated at the Battle of Adwa. Respect!

questions.question: How many time zones does South Africa have?
questions.explanation: SA has just 1 time zone: SAST (South African Standard Time), which is UTC+2. The whole country runs on the same clock!

questions.question: Which country is both in Europe AND Asia?
questions.explanation: Turkey spans both continents, with Istanbul straddling the divide. The Bosphorus strait separates the European and Asian parts.

questions.question: How many stars are on the flag of the United States?
questions.explanation: 50 stars for 50 states! The 13 stripes represent the original colonies. SA's flag has 6 colours though -- one of the most colourful in the world.

questions.question: Order these African countries from NORTH to SOUTH: (1) South Africa (2) Egypt (3) Kenya (4) Nigeria
questions.explanation: Egypt is the furthest north, then Nigeria in West Africa, Kenya on the east, and South Africa at the bottom. North to south: Egypt, Nigeria, Kenya, SA.

questions.question: Which is the only country in the world completely surrounded by South Africa?
questions.explanation: Lesotho is completely enclosed by South Africa -- it's an enclave! Eswatini also borders Mozambique, so it doesn't count.

questions.question: Roughly how many official languages does India have? (Give or take 2)
questions.explanation: India has 22 officially recognised languages in its constitution. And you thought SA's 11 was a lot!

questions.question: Which strait separates Africa from Europe?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Morocco (Africa) from Spain (Europe). It's only about 14 km wide at its narrowest!

questions.question: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
questions.explanation: Canada has the longest coastline at about 202,080 km, thanks to all those islands and fjords. SA's coastline is about 2,800 km -- just a fraction!

questions.question: Order these world cities from WEST to EAST: (1) Cape Town (2) London (3) Tokyo (4) New York
questions.explanation: Going west to east from the Prime Meridian: New York (-74), London (0), Cape Town (18), Tokyo (140). Order: New York, London, Cape Town, Tokyo.

questions.question: What percentage of the Earth's surface is covered by water?
questions.explanation: About 71% of Earth's surface is water. That's a LOT of ocean. And yet we still have water restrictions in Cape Town!

questions.question: How many landlocked countries are in Africa? (Within 3 either way)
questions.explanation: Africa has 16 landlocked countries, including Lesotho, Eswatini, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Zambia, Malawi, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, Ethiopia, South Sudan, Central African Republic, Chad, Niger, Mali, and Burkina Faso.

questions.question: Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa's tallest peak, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Kilimanjaro is in Tanzania, right near the Kenyan border. At 5,895m it's the tallest freestanding mountain in the world. Makes Drakensberg look like a speed bump!

questions.question: Approximately how many countries are members of the United Nations? (Within 5 either way)
questions.explanation: 193 member states in the UN. South Africa was a founding member in 1945, was suspended during apartheid, and fully rejoined in 1994.

questions.question: The Great Barrier Reef is found off the coast of which country?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is off the northeast coast of Australia. It's the world's largest coral reef system -- over 2,300 km long!

questions.question: Which African country has the largest economy by GDP?
questions.explanation: Nigeria has the largest economy in Africa, driven largely by its oil industry. SA is typically 2nd or 3rd. Size matters in economics!

questions.question: How many countries does the equator pass through? (Within 1 either way)
questions.explanation: The equator passes through 13 countries: Ecuador, Colombia, Brazil, Sao Tome & Principe, Gabon, Republic of Congo, DR Congo, Uganda, Kenya, Somalia, Maldives, Indonesia, and Kiribati.

questions.question: Which of these cities is CLOSEST to the equator?
questions.explanation: Quito sits almost exactly on the equator at about 0.18 degrees south! Nairobi is at 1.3S, Kinshasa at 4.3S, and Lagos at 6.5N.

questions.question: Which country has the most natural lakes in the world?
questions.explanation: Canada has over 800,000 natural lakes -- more than the rest of the world combined! All that glacier action from the ice ages.

questions.question: Order these deserts from SMALLEST to LARGEST area: (1) Sahara (2) Kalahari (3) Namib (4) Gobi
questions.explanation: Namib (~81,000 km2), Kalahari (~900,000 km2), Gobi (~1.3M km2), Sahara (~9.2M km2). Two of these deserts are right here in southern Africa!

questions.question: What is the driest continent on Earth?
questions.explanation: Antarctica! It's technically a desert -- it gets less than 200mm of precipitation per year in the interior. Colder than a Joburg winter but drier than the Karoo!

questions.question: How many countries in Africa have 'Guinea' in their name?
questions.explanation: Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, and Equatorial Guinea -- that's 3! (Papua New Guinea is in Oceania, so it doesn't count.)

questions.question: The Tropic of Capricorn passes through South Africa. Approximately how far south of the equator is it?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Capricorn is at approximately 23.5 degrees south. In SA, it passes through the Limpopo province.

questions.question: How many countries in the world are island nations with NO land border with any other country? (Within 5 either way)
questions.explanation: There are about 47 island nations with no land borders. These include Japan, Iceland, Madagascar, Cuba, Sri Lanka, and many Pacific island nations.

questions.question: Which country was formerly known as Rhodesia?
questions.explanation: Zimbabwe was called Rhodesia (named after Cecil John Rhodes) until independence in 1980. Zambia was Northern Rhodesia. A bit of southern African history for you!

questions.question: Which city is further north: Rome (Italy) or New York (USA)?
questions.explanation: Surprise! Rome is at about 41.9N while New York is at about 40.7N. Rome is slightly further north, even though it feels much warmer. The Mediterranean climate and Gulf Stream make all the difference!

questions.question: How many times does the International Date Line change the calendar date as you cross it? (Hint: it's always this many, regardless of direction)
questions.explanation: The International Date Line always changes the date by exactly 1 day -- you either gain or lose a day depending on direction. West to east: lose a day. East to west: gain a day.

questions.question: Which of these African countries does the Prime Meridian (0 degrees longitude) pass through?
questions.explanation: The Prime Meridian passes through Ghana (near Accra), as well as Algeria, Mali, Burkina Faso, and Togo in Africa. Ghana's location at 0 degrees is a cool geographic fact!

questions.question: The Mariana Trench is the deepest point in the ocean. Approximately how deep is it in kilometres? (Nearest whole number)
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench is about 11 km deep (10,994 metres). If you put Mount Everest at the bottom, there'd still be over 2 km of water above it!

questions.question: Order these world rivers from SHORTEST to LONGEST: (1) Amazon (2) Nile (3) Orange River (SA) (4) Thames (UK)
questions.explanation: Thames (346km), Orange River (2,200km), Amazon (6,400km), Nile (6,650km). Our Orange River is longer than you might think!

questions.question: Which two countries are doubly landlocked (surrounded entirely by landlocked countries)?
questions.explanation: Liechtenstein (surrounded by Switzerland and Austria) and Uzbekistan (surrounded by 5 landlocked countries) are the world's only doubly landlocked countries. You'd need to cross at least 2 borders to reach the sea!

questions.question: How many African countries have English as an official language? (Within 3 either way)
questions.explanation: About 20 African countries have English as an official language, including South Africa, Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Tanzania (with Swahili), and many more. The British Empire left quite a linguistic legacy!

questions.question: Which country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
questions.explanation: Italy leads with 59 UNESCO World Heritage Sites (as of 2024). China is close behind. South Africa has 10 -- including Robben Island and the Cradle of Humankind!

questions.question: What is the approximate flight distance in kilometres from Johannesburg to Sydney, Australia? (Within 500km)
questions.explanation: Joburg to Sydney is about 11,000 km as the crow flies. That's roughly an 11-12 hour direct flight. Long way to go watch the Springboks play!

questions.question: What is the only sea in the world with no coastline (no land borders)?
questions.explanation: The Sargasso Sea in the North Atlantic is bounded only by ocean currents, not by land! It's defined by the Gulf Stream and other currents. The Dead Sea and Caspian Sea are actually lakes.

questions.question: How many countries does Brazil share a land border with? (Hint: it's more than you think)
questions.explanation: Brazil borders 10 countries! French Guiana, Suriname, Guyana, Venezuela, Colombia, Peru, Bolivia, Paraguay, Argentina, and Uruguay. Every South American country except Chile and Ecuador!

questions.question: Which African country has the most pyramids?
questions.explanation: Sudan has about 200-255 pyramids compared to Egypt's 138! The ancient Nubian civilisation built many pyramids at Meroe. Most people assume Egypt, but Sudan takes the crown!

questions.question: How many time zones does Russia span?
questions.explanation: Russia spans 11 time zones! When it's midnight in Kaliningrad (west), it's 10am in Kamchatka (east). That's one massive country.

questions.question: Which country's capital city is the highest above sea level?
questions.explanation: La Paz, Bolivia sits at about 3,640m above sea level -- the highest capital city in the world. Quito is at 2,850m. For comparison, Joburg is at about 1,753m.

questions.question: Order these BRICS nations by population, SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) India (2) South Africa (3) Brazil (4) Russia
questions.explanation: SA (~60M), Russia (~144M), Brazil (~215M), India (~1.4B). South Africa is by far the smallest BRICS nation by population!

questions.question: How many African countries are members of the Commonwealth of Nations? (Within 2 either way)
questions.explanation: 21 African countries are Commonwealth members, including South Africa (which left in 1961 and rejoined in 1994), Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, Tanzania, and others. Most are former British colonies.

questions.question: What is the southernmost capital city in the world?
questions.explanation: Wellington, New Zealand at 41.3S is the southernmost capital city. Cape Town is SA's legislative capital at 33.9S but Wellington beats it! Buenos Aires is at 34.6S.

questions.question: The deepest lake in Africa is Lake Tanganyika. Approximately how deep is it in metres? (Within 100m)
questions.explanation: Lake Tanganyika is about 1,470 metres deep -- the second deepest lake in the world after Lake Baikal. It borders Tanzania, DR Congo, Burundi, and Zambia.

questions.question: Which country has the most official languages in the world?
questions.explanation: Bolivia has 37 official languages! That beats even India's 22 and Zimbabwe's 16. South Africa's 11 is impressive but Bolivia takes it to another level entirely.

questions.question: Point Nemo in the Pacific Ocean is the furthest point from any land on Earth. Approximately how far is it from the nearest coastline?
questions.explanation: Point Nemo is about 2,688 km from the nearest land (Ducie Island). It's so remote that the closest humans are often astronauts on the ISS overhead! Absolutely mind-blowing.
